data.title: Bitdeer Technologies Reports Significant Revenue Decline Amid Strategic Shift
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Bitdeer Technologies Group, a prominent Bitcoin mining company based in Singapore, has announced a substantial 41% decrease in revenue for the first quarter of 2025, totaling $70.1 million. This marks a significant drop from the previous year's figures, as the company reported an operating loss of $3.2 million, contrasting sharply with the $34.1 million profit recorded during the same period last year. Despite these challenges, Bitdeer achieved a net income exceeding $400 million, primarily attributed to gains from convertible notes and warrants issued to stablecoin issuer Tether in 2024.

Bitdeer's revenue decline reflects a broader trend among miners who are diversifying beyond Bitcoin mining to focus on high-performance computing (HPC) for artificial intelligence applications. Matt Kong, Bitdeer's chief business officer, emphasized the company's commitment to scaling self-mining operations and advancing plans for U.S.-based HPC and AI infrastructure. This strategic pivot comes in response to the Bitcoin network's halving event in April 2024, which significantly reduced mining revenue.

In an effort to counteract the decline in mining revenue, Bitdeer has been selling its energy-efficient Bitcoin mining hardware. However, these sales have yet to compensate for the lost income from traditional mining activities. The company is actively ramping up its self-mining operations, utilizing its mining hardware to accumulate Bitcoin directly. Bitdeer anticipates its self-mining hashrate to reach 40 exahashes per second (EH/s) by the end of 2025, a measure of the computing power securing the Bitcoin network. Kong expressed optimism about achieving rapid growth in self-mining hashrate, citing the swift production of SEALMINER mining rigs and ample global power capacity.

As of March, Tether held a 21% stake in Bitdeer, according to U.S. regulatory filings. The company is reportedly investing in its U.S. expansion as a strategic hedge against potential trade conflicts. This move underscores Bitdeer's proactive approach to navigating the evolving landscape of cryptocurrency mining and its commitment to leveraging technological advancements to sustain growth and profitability.

data.title: FTX estate to start distributing more than $5B on May 30
data.description:
More than two-and-a-half years after filing for bankruptcy, cryptocurrency exchange FTX is moving forward with repaying users who have not had access to their funds. In a May 15 notice, the FTX Recovery Trust announced that it would begin disbursing funds to the second group of parties eligible under the exchange’s reorganization plan. Starting on May 30, FTX will send more than $5 billion to creditors “within 1 to 3 business days” through crypto firms BitGo and Kraken.  In accordance with the reorganization plan, FTX said five groups of “convenience classes” would receive between 54% and 120% distribution of assets
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/ftx-second-distribution-starting?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Stablecoin Regulation Poised to Drive Institutional Interest in Crypto Industry
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, stablecoin regulation is emerging as a pivotal factor for the cryptocurrency industry, potentially sparking significant interest from institutional investors. Ash Pampati, head of ecosystem at the Aptos Foundation, emphasized the importance of stablecoins during an interview at Consensus 2025 in Toronto. He noted that while the global community outside the United States has embraced stablecoins, the U.S. is on the brink of doing so. Pampati highlighted the borderless nature and efficiency of stablecoins, particularly the dollar onchain, as a catalyst for new use cases. He questioned the necessity of complex processes for sending money internationally, such as to Nigeria, when stablecoins offer a streamlined alternative.

Stablecoins are increasingly utilized for cross-border transactions due to their cost-effectiveness compared to traditional financial methods like wire transfers. They also serve as a hedge against fiat currency volatility, which is prevalent in emerging markets. A survey conducted by Fireblocks reveals that Latin America is leading in stablecoin adoption, with 71% of respondents utilizing the technology for international payments. Additionally, half of the respondents in the region anticipate stablecoins to reduce transaction costs compared to conventional financial systems. Pampati expressed optimism about the growing interest from institutional investors, suggesting that stablecoins could revolutionize the fintech landscape across business-to-business and business-to-consumer sectors with fully onchain solutions.

The Fireblocks survey indicates that 86% of respondents believe their companies are prepared to integrate stablecoins, demonstrating infrastructure readiness. Furthermore, 75% of respondents acknowledge clear customer demand for stablecoins. Despite the technological advancements, regulatory frameworks remain crucial in shaping stablecoin adoption. The survey highlights increasing confidence in stablecoins, driven not only by technological benefits but also by diminishing regulatory hurdles. Global regulatory efforts have been underway, with notable progress such as the European Union's MiCA regulation, initiatives in the United Arab Emirates, and the United States' GENIUS Act, which has reportedly regained bipartisan support following a previous setback. These developments underscore the evolving landscape of stablecoin regulation and its potential impact on the cryptocurrency industry.

data.title: Meta Platforms Delays AI Model Release, Sparking Concerns Over Investment Strategy
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Meta Platforms has postponed the release of its flagship AI model, raising internal concerns about the direction of its multi-billion dollar AI investments. Insiders revealed that engineers are striving to enhance the performance of the large language model named 'Behemoth,' while employees question whether the improvements are sufficient for a public launch. Initially scheduled to debut at Meta's first AI developer conference in April, the release was delayed to June and is now postponed until autumn or later.

Despite Meta's previous accolades for rapidly catching up with competitors like OpenAI and investing $72 billion to fulfill CEO Mark Zuckerberg's AI ambitions, the training of 'Behemoth' has encountered obstacles, resulting in performance falling short of expectations. The executive team has expressed dissatisfaction with the Llama 4 development group and is considering a management restructuring within the AI product department.

data.title: Federal Reserve Warns of Economic Risks from Tariff-Related Disruptions
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Federal Reserve official Barr stated that while the U.S. economy remains fundamentally strong, there are concerns about potential disruptions in the supply chain due to tariffs, which could slow economic growth and increase inflation. Barr highlighted the critical role of small businesses in the supply chain and the broader economy.

He noted that trade policies have cast uncertainty over the economic outlook. For small businesses, potential supply chain disruptions are particularly severe, partly because they have fewer opportunities to access credit. Barr added that small businesses often provide specialized inputs that are not easily sourced elsewhere, and their closure could further disrupt supply chains.

data.title: Russia Deploys 640,000 Troops to Ukraine Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: According to BlockBeats, market reports indicate that Russia has deployed 640,000 soldiers to Ukraine. This development marks a significant escalation in the ongoing conflict between the two nations. The deployment comes amid heightened tensions and international scrutiny over the situation in the region. The move is expected to have substantial implications for the geopolitical landscape and may influence future diplomatic efforts to resolve the crisis.

data.title: Bitcoin to outperform gold in second half of 2025: JP Morgan
data.description:
Gold has been losing steam recently after it raced to record highs due to geopolitical and economic uncertainties, including tariffs. Bitcoin has been the winner, with gold being the loser in a hedge trade against currencies. The increasing number of US states and companies buying bitcoin will be another catalyst
source: https://coinjournal.net/news/bitcoin-to-outperform-gold-in-second-half-of-2025-jp-morgan/
